title: Photo mounting — corners, adhesives, and conservation options
sources: SYNTHESISED — conservation mounting practice is well-documented in archival literature; no single PD canonical source
notes: Covers: photo corners (archival corners vs paper-fold DIY corners), dry adhesives, PVA spot-mounting, and full-float mounting on a matboard window. Conservation distinction: archival mounting allows removal of the photograph undamaged; non-archival adhesive is permanent. For heirloom albums, recommends corners only or reversible methyl-cellulose spots.
